plain_definition: The mandatory disclosure document MiCA requires before most crypto-assets (including asset-referenced tokens and e-money tokens) are offered to the public or admitted to trading in the EU -- setting out required information about the issuer, the project, the offer's terms, the rights attached to the token, the underlying technology, and the risks involved. For an asset-referenced token it must be approved by a national competent authority before publication; for most other crypto-assets it need only be notified to the relevant NCA. It plays a similar disclosure role to a securities prospectus, but is specific to MiCA's own crypto-asset categories.
formal_definition: MiCA's own EUR-Lex summary states that issuers must 'publish a crypto-asset White Paper and any marketing communication on their website'; the detailed content requirements sit in MiCA Articles 6-15 (general crypto-assets) and the equivalent Title III/IV provisions for asset-referenced and e-money tokens (Regulation (EU) 2023/1114, OJ L 150, 9.6.2023).
